metadata.readIf: You are starting, routing, or landing work in this repository.
metadata.scope: Repository-wide agent workflow, intake usage, and verification expectations.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Agent workflow and handoff policy for vibeSpatial.
metadata.stopIf: You only need a narrow API detail already covered by a routed doc.

risks: Reading the whole repo before routing increases context noise and slows edits.
Silent CPU fallback changes can ship if runtime docs and tests drift apart.
Stale generated headers or intake index can misroute future tasks.
docs.title: Agent Instructions

metadata.readIf: You are changing variant selection, NVML monitoring, adaptive chunking, or runtime planning.
metadata.scope: Probe-first adaptive planning, monitoring inputs, and chunk-boundary runtime decisions.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-1 adaptive runtime policy before broader kernel work lands.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has a settled adaptive-runtime contract and only needs implementation detail.

risks: A full live controller would create more machinery than value before real kernels and chunked workloads exist.
Overfitting variant choice too early can freeze bad metadata into the registry contract.
Hard-coding NVML into call sites would make later telemetry upgrades expensive.
docs.title: Adaptive Runtime

metadata.readIf: You are changing public method delegation, dispatch events, or GeoPandas adapter wiring.
metadata.scope: GeoPandas method delegation, dispatch events, and object-construction avoidance.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: API dispatch policy for GeoPandas-facing method boundaries.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has the adapter implementation open and only needs local detail.

risks: Reconstructing wrapper arrays inside delegate helpers adds avoidable overhead per call.
Caching owned conversions without invalidation on mutation leads to stale state.
Dispatch events that materialize host buffers defeat the purpose of lightweight metadata.
docs.title: Api-Dispatch

metadata.readIf: You are changing exact predicate kernels, binary predicate dispatch, or GeoPandas predicate integration.
metadata.scope: Exact binary-predicate refine strategy, coarse-filter staging, and GeoPandas adapter policy.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-4 exact binary-predicate architecture policy before join assembly.
metadata.stopIf: You already have the binary-predicate engine open and only need local implementation detail.

risks: A monolithic exact kernel would hide the compaction boundaries needed for CCCL and later GPU fallback staging.
Host-side small-array overhead can erase any value from coarse filtering if the adapter never short-circuits.
Predicate semantics will drift from GeoPandas if the vendored array path and the internal kernel path diverge.
docs.title: Binary Predicate Refine Pipeline
verify: uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_geom_methods.py -k "contains or within or intersects or covers or covered_by or touches or crosses"

VIBESPATIAL_STRICT_NATIVE=1 uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_array.py -k "test_predicates_vector_scalar or test_predicates_vector_vector or test_chaining or test_raise_on_bad_sizes" -q

metadata.readIf: You are changing clip_by_rect, rectangle clip performance, or early constructive fast paths.
metadata.scope: Rectangle clip fast-path strategy, owned constructive dataflow, and GeoPandas adapter policy.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-5 rectangle clip fast-path policy before broader overlay assembly.
metadata.stopIf: You already have the rectangle clip engine open and only need local implementation detail.

risks: General polygon intersection is too broad for the first constructive fast path.
Forcing a slower owned host implementation onto GeoPandas would ship a performance regression.
Hole, multipolygon, and invalid-input behavior can drift if the fast path is not checked against the degeneracy corpus.
docs.title: Rectangle Clip Fast Paths
verify: uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tools/tests/test_clip.py -k "test_clip_poly or test_clip_line_keep_slivers or test_clip_multipoly_keep_slivers"

metadata.readIf: You are changing auto mode, size thresholds, or CPU/GPU dispatch heuristics.
metadata.scope: Per-kernel auto-dispatch thresholds and CPU/GPU crossover policy.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-1 crossover policy before adaptive runtime lands.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has a settled crossover policy and only needs implementation detail.

risks: A single threshold would hide the real differences between bounds, predicates, and overlay work.
Dispatching small inputs to GPU can lose badly to single-threaded GeoPandas.
Leaving crossover implicit invites ad hoc size checks inside individual kernels.
docs.title: Dispatch Crossover Policy

metadata.readIf: You are changing reproducibility behavior, reduction order, or deterministic mode for GPU-oriented kernels.
metadata.scope: Deterministic versus performance-first runtime policy, reproducibility guarantees, and reduction-order rules.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-2 determinism and reproducibility policy before broader GPU reductions land.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has the determinism policy module open and only needs local implementation detail.

risks: Claiming cross-device reproducibility would be false; different architectures
Hidden nondeterminism in reductions, scans, or floating atomics makes
Forcing deterministic order everywhere would waste performance on operations
docs.title: Determinism And Reproducibility

metadata.readIf: You are changing dissolve, grouped union, or segmented attribute aggregation.
metadata.scope: Grouped dissolve pipeline staging, segmented union, and attribute aggregation policy.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Dissolve pipeline architecture for grouped constructive work.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has the dissolve pipeline open and only needs local implementation detail.

risks: Python group iteration dominates wall time if the group-span stage is not bulk.
Stable in-group row order is required for deterministic output.
Replacing the full public method instead of just the grouped union stage overcomplicates the GPU transition.
docs.title: Dissolve

metadata.readIf: You are changing fallback visibility, host-only geospatial paths, or runtime observability.
metadata.scope: Observable CPU fallback policy for unsupported predicates, geometry mixes, and host-only query paths.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-4 explicit fallback policy for predicate and spatial-query surfaces.
metadata.stopIf: You already have the fallback event API open and only need local implementation detail.

risks: Silent host execution makes it impossible to tell whether a GPU path exists.
Warning-based fallback can pollute upstream behavior if used as the only visibility channel.
Fallback logs are not useful if they omit the surface and the reason.
docs.title: Explicit Fallback Surfaces
verify: uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_array.py -k "contains or within or intersects"

metadata.readIf: You are changing pipeline execution, fused chains, or intermediate materialization behavior.
metadata.scope: Kernel-fusion policy, stage boundaries, and intermediate elimination rules.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-2 fusion strategy before broad multi-kernel pipelines land.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has a settled fusion contract and only needs implementation detail.

risks: A full lazy graph would add scheduling complexity before the repo has enough kernels to justify it.
Only relying on hand-written fused kernels would leave too much performance on the table and duplicate logic.
Fusing across the wrong boundaries can hide reusable structures or break residency and diagnostics guarantees.
docs.title: Fusion Strategy

metadata.readIf: You are designing adapters, kernels, or memory layout for owned geometry arrays.
metadata.scope: Canonical owned geometry buffer schema for points, lines, polygons, and multiparts.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-2 owned geometry buffer layout contract.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has a settled buffer schema and only needs implementation detail.

risks: Choosing an eager object-like layout now would force expensive rewrites once kernels want contiguous payloads.
Overfitting the schema to one geometry family would make mixed arrays and multipart kernels awkward later.
Mixing canonical storage concerns with execution-local staging would blur residency and precision boundaries.
docs.title: Geometry Buffer Schema

metadata.readIf: You are changing GeoArrow, GeoParquet, WKB, GeoJSON, or Shapefile performance strategy or decode architecture.
metadata.scope: Post-Phase-6b GPU-native IO execution model, staged decode policy, and format-level speed targets.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: IO acceleration policy for turning repo-owned adapters into GPU-dominant ingest and emission paths.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has the routed IO implementation files open and only needs local adapter detail.

risks: Treating every format as bespoke work will fragment the fast path and dilute GPU effort.
Decoding before pruning will erase most of the potential GeoParquet win.
A generic mixed-family decoder will drag homogeneous fast paths down to the slow case.
Text and legacy container support can quietly reintroduce per-row Python work if not measured.
docs.title: Io-Acceleration
verify: uv run python scripts/intake.py "gpu native io acceleration roadmap"

metadata.readIf: You are changing Arrow, GeoParquet, WKB adapters, or owned-buffer IO decode and encode.
metadata.scope: Arrow, GeoParquet, and WKB IO boundary around owned geometry buffers and GPU-native decode paths.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: IO architecture for Arrow, GeoParquet, and WKB owned-buffer bridges.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has the specific IO adapter open and only needs local implementation detail.

risks: Repeatedly rebuilding Shapely-heavy intermediate state in the Arrow path destroys throughput.
Silent host decode hides missing GPU paths.
WKB compatibility bridge becoming the de facto layout instead of GeoArrow.
docs.title: Io-Arrow

metadata.readIf: You are changing read_file, to_file, GeoJSON ingest, Shapefile ingest, or file-format routing.
metadata.scope: File-based vector format routing for GeoJSON, Shapefile, and legacy GDAL adapters.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: File-format IO architecture for GeoJSON, Shapefile, and GDAL legacy adapters.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has the specific format adapter open and only needs local implementation detail.

risks: Legacy GDAL formats masquerading as native hides work that bypasses the GPU stack.
GeoJSON geometry ingest is now GPU-accelerated (ADR-0038); remaining bottleneck is CPU property extraction.
Shapefile adapter losing speed leadership if the raw Arrow-binary fast path regresses.
docs.title: Io-Files

metadata.readIf: You are classifying a format pathway or adding a new IO format target.
metadata.scope: IO format classification as GPU-native, hybrid, or fallback-only.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: IO format classification matrix for GPU-native versus hybrid versus fallback paths.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has the specific format adapter open and only needs local implementation detail.

risks: Compatibility formats becoming the design center instead of GeoArrow and GeoParquet.
Missing explicit fallback events on legacy GDAL formats hides work that bypasses the GPU stack.
docs.title: Io-Support-Matrix

metadata.readIf: You are changing make_valid, validity checking, or topology repair pipelines.
metadata.scope: Compact-invalid-row make_valid pipeline staging and repair-only-invalids policy.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Make-valid pipeline architecture for compact-and-repair staging.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has the make_valid pipeline open and only needs local implementation detail.

risks: Running repair on all rows instead of compacted invalids wastes compute on already-valid geometries.
Validity checking and repair becoming coupled prevents staging them as separate GPU stages.
docs.title: Make-Valid

metadata.readIf: You are designing owned geometry buffers, mixed-type dispatch, or geometry-family layout policy.
metadata.scope: Mixed-geometry storage and execution strategy for GPU-oriented geometry buffers.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-1 design decision for mixed-geometry handling before buffer implementation.
metadata.stopIf: Your task is limited to a single kernel implementation detail that already has a chosen layout.

risks: Canonical storage and execution strategy are easy to conflate; they should not be the same decision.
GeometryCollections remain a pathological mixed-type case and should not force the common fast path.
A storage model that optimizes memory at ingest can still lose badly once divergent kernels dominate runtime.
docs.title: Mixed Geometries

metadata.readIf: You are defining missing-geometry behavior, validity handling, or empty-geometry contracts.
metadata.scope: Null versus empty geometry semantics, validity rules, and kernel-state policy.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-1 null and empty geometry policy before owned kernel expansion.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has a settled null and empty contract and only needs implementation detail.

risks: Treating null and empty as the same state breaks GeoPandas and Shapely semantics.
Scalar branching for null or empty cases can create avoidable warp divergence.
Buffer layouts that omit validity information will force slow-path recovery later.
docs.title: Null And Empty Geometry Semantics

metadata.readIf: You are changing union, difference, symmetric difference, or overlay output reconstruction.
metadata.scope: Overlay reconstruction staging, face-labeling plan, and CCCL-oriented output assembly policy.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-5 reconstruction plan from segment primitives to public overlay outputs.
metadata.stopIf: You already have the reconstruction planner open and only need local implementation detail.

risks: Monolithic overlay kernels would hide the exact stage boundaries needed for CCCL.
If stable ordering is not explicit, later dissolve and overlay assembly will drift from pandas-facing semantics.
Union, difference, and symmetric difference should not each invent separate topology assembly logic.
docs.title: Overlay Reconstruction

metadata.readIf: You are changing point predicates, candidate refinement, or the first exact spatial query path.
metadata.scope: Point-versus-bounds and point-in-polygon pipeline shape, stage boundaries, and fallback contract.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-4 point predicate architecture policy before sindex and join assembly.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has the staged point-predicate contract open and only needs local implementation detail.

risks: Monolithic predicate kernels would bypass the coarse-filter and adaptive-runtime work already landed.
Silent CPU fallback would hide the absence of a real GPU predicate variant.
Predicate boundary semantics can drift if bounds checks and exact refine are tested separately.
docs.title: Point Predicate Pipeline

metadata.readIf: You are designing kernel arithmetic, precision selection, or numerical policy.
metadata.scope: Dual fp32/fp64 compute strategy, runtime precision dispatch, and canonical storage policy.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-1 precision dispatch policy before owned kernel expansion.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has a settled precision plan and only needs implementation detail.

risks: Canonical fp32 storage would trade away too much accuracy for geographic and projected coordinates.
Native fp64 everywhere would erase the performance advantage on consumer GPUs.
A single global precision switch would hide kernel-class differences that matter for correctness.
docs.title: Precision Strategy

metadata.readIf: You are designing buffer movement, interop adapters, or host/device materialization behavior.
metadata.scope: Device residency defaults, transfer visibility rules, and zero-copy interop policy.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-1 residency and transfer policy before owned geometry buffers land.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has a settled residency contract and only needs implementation detail.

risks: Silent host copies can erase GPU wins while still looking correct in tests.
Early buffer work can lock in unnecessary copies if residency is underspecified.
Interop wrappers can accidentally materialize on host unless zero-copy rules are explicit.
docs.title: Residency Policy

metadata.readIf: You are designing predicate math, intersection logic, or overlay correctness guarantees.
metadata.scope: Exactness guarantees, fallback policy, and topology-preservation strategy for predicates and overlay.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-1 robustness policy for predicate and constructive kernels.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has a settled robustness contract and only needs implementation detail.

risks: Wrong orientation or intersection signs will silently poison every later predicate and overlay kernel.
Exact-style fallback that diverges per row can destroy GPU efficiency if it is not staged carefully.
Snap or grid policies can preserve topology while still changing geometry semantics if applied indiscriminately.
docs.title: Robustness Strategy

metadata.readIf: You are changing runtime selection, GPU execution, fallback visibility, or kernels.
metadata.scope: GPU-first runtime rules, fallback policy, and execution invariants.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Runtime architecture policy for GPU-first execution.
metadata.stopIf: Your task is docs-only or limited to vendored test maintenance.

risks: Silent CPU fallback hides unsupported GPU behavior.
Runtime-selection changes can desync the GeoPandas shim from the runtime layer.
Kernel-oriented changes can look correct locally while breaking the upstream contract.
docs.title: Runtime Model

metadata.readIf: You are changing segment intersection math, degeneracy handling, or constructive-kernel foundations.
metadata.scope: Robust segment extraction, candidate generation, and exact intersection-classification strategy for constructive kernels.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-5 segment intersection primitive policy before overlay assembly.
metadata.stopIf: You already have the segment primitive implementation open and only need local implementation detail.

risks: Wrong segment classification poisons every later overlay kernel.
Exact arithmetic on every row would destroy GPU throughput.
Routing candidate generation through materialized Shapely geometry would lock Phase 5 to the host path we are trying to replace.
docs.title: Segment Intersection Primitives

metadata.readIf: You are changing sindex query behavior, spatial join materialization, dwithin, or nearest result assembly.
metadata.scope: Spatial-index query assembly, sjoin result semantics, bounded nearest strategy, and output-format policy.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-4 spatial query and join assembly policy before broader API dispatch work.
metadata.stopIf: You already have the spatial-query engine and vendored join helpers open and only need local implementation detail.

risks: Query/index speedups are meaningless if join assembly breaks index names, suffixes, or geometry-column retention.
A nearest implementation without a bounded-search fast path will not scale.
Falling back to Shapely/STRtree for unsupported inputs must stay explicit enough that future GPU work is not hidden.
docs.title: Spatial Query And Join Assembly
verify: uv run python scripts/profile_fixture_spatial_query.py --fixture polygons-regular-grid-rows100000 --operation query --ensure

metadata.readIf: You are changing buffer, offset_curve, or stroke-style constructive kernels.
metadata.scope: Buffer and offset-curve kernel seam, prefix-sum emission strategy, and Shapely fallback policy.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Stroke kernel architecture for buffer and offset-curve constructive work.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has the stroke kernel implementation open and only needs local implementation detail.

risks: Current host prototypes are 4-5x slower than Shapely; public dispatch must stay on Shapely fallback until GPU variants land.
Prefix-sum emission complexity grows with join and cap classification; land simple cases first.
docs.title: Stroke-Kernels

metadata.readIf: You are resolving, revisiting, or querying a design decision.
metadata.scope: Architecture decision log and index of accepted or superseded ADRs.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Decision log for architecture choices.
metadata.stopIf: You already have the specific ADR open and do not need the index.

risks: Decisions can drift from implemented code if follow-up changes do not update the log.
Buried or unindexed ADRs make agents re-litigate settled design choices.
docs.title: Decision Log

metadata.readIf: You received a task request and need to decide what to open first.
metadata.scope: Operational intake workflow and request-to-doc routing policy.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Intake workflow and routing rules for agents in this repo.
metadata.stopIf: You already have a confirmed work plan and relevant docs open.

risks: Over-reading before classification slows work and hides the local contract.
Generated headers or intake index can drift if doc changes are not refreshed.
Cross-cutting requests still need judgment; intake should narrow the start, not replace engineering review.
docs.title: Intake Router

metadata.readIf: You are promoting, inspecting, or verifying upstream contract test groups.
metadata.scope: Upstream GeoPandas contract promotion gates, test-group ordering, and promotion workflow.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Contract promotion workflow for upstream GeoPandas test groups.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has the specific test group open and only needs local pass criteria.

risks: Promoting a contract group before smoke commands and tracked pass criteria are both explicit.
Mistaking contract promotion for GPU-performance completion.
Adding promotion gates that depend on optional runtime dependencies not available in CI.
docs.title: Contract-Promotion
verify: uv run python scripts/contract_promotion.py check vibeSpatial-o17.7.1

metadata.readIf: You are adding or verifying degeneracy cases for overlay, clip, or constructive kernels.
metadata.scope: Deterministic degeneracy corpus for overlay, clip, and segment-regression verification.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-5 degeneracy corpus policy for overlay and clip verification.
metadata.stopIf: You already have the corpus module open and only need local case detail.

risks: Random invalid-shape generators are useful for fuzzing but poor regression fixtures.
Overlay and clip can diverge on invalid inputs if the corpus does not record both expectations.
Missing hole, touching-ring, or null/empty cases leads to false confidence in later GPU kernels.
docs.title: Degeneracy Corpus

metadata.readIf: You are adding or verifying IO benchmark rails, throughput floors, or format-specific performance gates.
metadata.scope: IO benchmark rail suites, throughput floors, and format-level performance enforcement.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-6d IO performance rail and floor enforcement workflow.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has the benchmark scripts open and only needs local implementation detail.

risks: Drift in performance baselines if the rail does not run regularly.
GeoJSON informational-only status masking real regression.
Throughput floors set too low to catch meaningful regressions.
docs.title: IO Performance Rails

metadata.readIf: You are generating, reviewing, or extending owned kernel scaffolds.
metadata.scope: Scaffolded kernel inventory, benchmark stubs, and generated test surfaces.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Kernel scaffold inventory and generated surface map for repo-owned kernels.
metadata.stopIf: You already have the target kernel module and generated files open.

risks: Scaffold drift can leave tests, benchmarks, and manifests out of sync.
Tier metadata becomes meaningless if generated benchmarks do not match policy.
docs.title: Kernel Inventory

metadata.readIf: You are measuring, reporting, or verifying strict native GeoPandas compatibility.
metadata.scope: Strict native mode upstream test pass rate as the real GeoPandas compatibility metric.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Native coverage metric definition for upstream GeoPandas compatibility.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has the coverage script open and only needs the command.

risks: Missing optional dependencies inflate skipped count, making the primary metric misleading.
Host fallback counting as a pass in non-strict mode hides real coverage gaps.
docs.title: Native-Coverage

metadata.readIf: You are defining kernel success criteria, benchmark rails, or performance gates.
metadata.scope: Performance tier gates, reference datasets, and benchmark acceptance policy.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-0 performance gate policy for GPU kernel work.
metadata.stopIf: Your task is limited to a single benchmark implementation detail already routed elsewhere.

risks: Speedup targets can become meaningless if the reference denominator shifts.
Small synthetic cases can overstate GPU wins by hiding transfer and setup costs.
Gates that are too strict too early can block correct-but-incomplete kernel landings.
docs.title: Performance Tiers
verify: uv run python scripts/intake.py "define performance tier gates for GPU kernels"

metadata.readIf: You are changing pipeline benchmarks, regression gates, or CPU/GPU movement profiling in CI.
metadata.scope: End-to-end pipeline benchmark suites, regression thresholds, and CI artifact workflow.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-1 pipeline benchmark and regression-gate workflow for end-to-end performance tracking.
metadata.stopIf: You already have the benchmark scripts open and only need a local implementation detail.

risks: Comparing current results to a stale or missing baseline can hide regressions
Reporting planner-selected GPU instead of actual hybrid execution hides where
Single-run timings are noisy; median-over-repeats is the local source of
docs.title: Pipeline Benchmarks

metadata.readIf: You are profiling join or overlay kernels, adding benchmark rails, or trying to explain where time is going.
metadata.scope: Stage-timed profiling entrypoints, NVTX guidance, and trace interpretation for join and overlay work.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Stage-level profiling workflow for join and overlay kernel development.
metadata.stopIf: You already have the profiling script open and only need a local implementation detail.

risks: Reporting planned GPU selection instead of actual execution device hides CPU
End-to-end timers alone blur sort, filter, and refine costs together.
Profiling rails that are not machine-readable are hard to diff and easy to
docs.title: Profiling Rails

metadata.readIf: You are adding benchmark data, regression corpora, or synthetic geometry fixtures.
metadata.scope: Synthetic geometry dataset generator workflow, shapes, and verification guidance.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-0 synthetic data generator contract for tests and benchmarks.
metadata.stopIf: Your task already has a routed generator API file open and needs no workflow guidance.

risks: Large preset sizes can exhaust memory if generated eagerly in broad test runs.
Optional GeoParquet export depends on `pyarrow` and should stay out of default smoke paths.
Synthetic shapes can drift away from benchmark policy if dataset families are added ad hoc.
docs.title: Synthetic Data

metadata.readIf: You are choosing which vendored test groups to promote or implement next.
metadata.scope: Ranked upstream GeoPandas contract slices, dependencies, and runtime weight.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Phase-0 upstream test inventory for implementation planning.
metadata.stopIf: Your task is limited to a single already-routed upstream module.

risks: Collected node counts do not measure wall-clock runtime directly.
Optional-dependency groups can appear artificially light when collection is skipped.
Promoting high-weight slices too early can hide narrower kernel milestones.
docs.title: Upstream Test Inventory
verify: uv run python scripts/intake.py "inventory upstream tests by weight dependency group and runtime"

metadata.readIf: You are refreshing, debugging, or expanding vendored upstream test coverage.
metadata.scope: Upstream GeoPandas test vendoring and contract-coverage workflow.
metadata.sourceOfTruth: Vendored upstream test workflow for GeoPandas compatibility.
metadata.stopIf: Your task does not touch vendored tests or compatibility contracts.

risks: Hand-editing vendored files makes upstream refreshes harder to replay.
Import rewrites can silently drift if the vendoring script changes without a smoke run.
Collection-only success does not guarantee runtime parity for changed fixtures or helpers.
docs.title: Upstream Tests
